body:
Audit source: 2026-06-11 axiomatic-shortcut pass.

`ArkLib/ProofSystem/Whir/KeystoneReduction.lean` contains a named structural anchor whose proposition is literally `True`:

- `def FoldedStackOfRound : Prop := True` — `ArkLib/ProofSystem/Whir/KeystoneReduction.lean:336`

This is not an asserted theorem, but it is a vacuous named proposition in the WHIR keystone/sumcheck bridge area. The surrounding comments say it should name the folded word stack that the keystone speaks about; the current definition does not carry that data.

Acceptance criteria:

- Replace `FoldedStackOfRound` with a semantic predicate carrying the round stack/domain/challenge data needed by `RoundProxGapBoundedByKeystone`, or remove it if it is unused.
- If retained, add theorem(s) proving the predicate for the concrete WHIR round construction.
- Ensure WHIR soundness bridge statements do not consume a `True` structural anchor.
- Validate the focused WHIR files and keep `python scripts/forbidden_tokens.py` clean.

Related broader WHIR tracking: #302.
title: WHIR: replace FoldedStackOfRound True structural anchor
